mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real GDP and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in aggregate demand and construction investment, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a win rate above 52% over 60-day horizons, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing peak efficiency, which compresses input costs and boosts short-term equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of late-cycle observations, or the mechanism alpha decays to zero when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5 percent imposes financing constraints on highly leveraged firms and reduces equity risk appetite, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 58% of high-rate periods, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total employment and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market deterioration that suppresses corporate revenue growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a positive 60-day return in more than 55% of cases, or the correlation between the two labor series breaks down without predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real personal consumption expenditures directly boost corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no directional bias during consumption expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline index of 100 during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply-side bottlenecks that erode profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of post-shock observations, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in recovery regimes.
